title: Ozoroa marginata
taxonomy: Ozoroa marginata is classified under the family Anacardiaceae, which is a group of plants commonly known as the cashew or poison ivy family. This classification suggests that Ozoroa marginata shares characteristics with other members of the Anacardiaceae family. Within the Anacardiaceae family, Ozoroa marginata is classified under the genus Ozoroa, which includes several species of shrubs or small trees native to southern Africa. Ozoroa marginata is further classified as a species within the Ozoroa genus, specifically Ozoroa marginata. This classification helps to identify and differentiate Ozoroa marginata from other plants within the Anacardiaceae family.

intro: Ozoroa marginata, also known as the white milkwood, is a small evergreen tree native to southern Africa, belonging to the family Anacardiaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Ozoroa marginata include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
